summary: Clio ranks highest because it owns regulated legal workflow, trusted records, and accountable system behavior. Figma is resilient through design-system context and developer handoff. Notion is improving fast with enterprise search, connectors, custom agents, and MCP governance, but remains a less uniformly mission-critical coordination layer.

quadrant.placement: Clio: Resilient; Figma: Resilient; Notion: Convenience near the resilient boundary
quadrant.rationale: Clio benefits from category-specific workflow ownership in a regulated domain. Figma benefits from collaborative design and handoff context. Notion owns useful workspace context and agent-ready infrastructure, but its workflow criticality is less consistent across customers.

Clio: Deep workflow embed and record ownership
Model improvements strengthen the system rather than bypass it
Figma: Strong distribution into design and development

Clio: Agents could route around interfaces if Clio does not remain the action layer
Figma: Human taste and product judgment remain external
Notion: Some AI experiences are reproducible by model-native products
Context is valuable but often portable

Clio: Push AI deeper into workflow automation and revenue capture
Keep agents grounded in permissions and firm data
Figma: Make design context indispensable to developers and agents
Own more of the design-to-code execution loop
Notion: Turn workspace context into deeper operational ownership
Make agent actions more trusted and workflow-native

final_verdict: Clio is the strongest resilience candidate of the three, Figma is a durable workflow platform with exposed generative layers, and Notion is improving quickly but still sits closer to a valuable coordination layer than a hard system of record.

confidence.reason: Official evidence is strong on product direction, integrations, governance, and security posture, but some workflow-depth and switching-cost judgments remain inferential.

comparison.relative_summary: Clio leads because it owns a regulated operating workflow with strong trust and accountability. Figma is more resilient than a generative design wrapper because it controls shared design-system and developer-handoff context. Notion is increasingly agent-ready and gains directly from better models, but its workflow criticality is less consistent.
key_differences: Clio's advantage is category-specific workflow ownership in a high-liability domain.
Figma's advantage is strong collaborative design context plus developer handoff.
Notion's advantage is broad workspace context and enterprise AI governance, but with weaker direct outcome ownership.
